description:
Goal
Model list reconstruction reuse as a first-class physical capability in compiler2 so unnecessary cons allocation disappears by construction, not by ad-hoc codegen patches.

Problem statement
Compiler2 currently extracts list heads/tails in native lowering, but the physical cons cell that produced the head is not carried as an explicit capability through compiler-generated continuations. The existing shared IR has a narrow `OwnedConsReuse { head }` capability and native codegen has a tail-only `emit_owned_cons_reuse_or_alloc` path, but that only handles `[same_head | new_tail]`. It cannot express true map-like reconstruction where the output head changes, and it is too easy for the capability to be lost at continuation/helper boundaries.

North star model
A reusable cons cell is a physical fact, not a semantic fact:

- `SplitList { source, head, tail }` proves that semantic value `head` came from physical cons cell `source`.
- A later construction `[new_head | new_tail]` may reuse `source` if the compiler can transport that physical source to the construction point.
- Runtime alias checks remain authoritative. Compiler facts are reuse candidates, not unsafe uniqueness proofs.
- Physical values must never participate in source semantics, activation identity, type inference, dispatch, or cross-process state.
- Hidden physical captures belong at the end of continuation/helper parameter lists so semantic parameters stay stable and physical erasure remains obvious.

Prior art and inspiration
The FP2 paper shows the right abstraction: model ownership/reuse credits explicitly and lower allocation into in-place update when safe. Old-world compiler code is useful for inspiration because it transported owned-cons sources through continuation captures, but do not wholesale import old-world planner/CPS structures. Compiler2 already has better seams:

- `src/compiler2/identity.rs`: `ExecutableKey { activation, need }` already separates semantic activation from delivery specialization. Do not add physical reuse to `ActivationKey`.
- `src/fz_ir/mod.rs`: `FnIr.physical_entry_params` and `FnIr.physical_capabilities` already erase physical params from semantic specialization.
- `src/compiler2/jobs/native.rs`: `BackendStep::SplitList` is the fact birth point.
- `src/compiler2/native_codegen/prim.rs`: `Prim::MakeList` and `Prim::DestListCons` are the fact consumption points.
- `runtime/src/heap/imp.rs` and `runtime/src/any_value.rs`: list cons allocation/layout already stores head raw bits, head kind, and tail bits; generalized reuse belongs here.

Signal
The work is correct only when:

- runtime tests prove unaliased source cons cells are rewritten in place and aliased/published cons cells allocate fresh without mutating the original;
- compiler2 native lowering emits/restores hidden physical params for reusable cons sources while semantic keys ignore them;
- native codegen consumes one generalized reusable-cons fact for `MakeList` and `DestListCons` instead of the current tail-only special case;
- `--log-telemetry` exposes reuse facts born, transported, consumed, and runtime fallback allocations;
- fixture contracts around `fixtures2/behavior/enum_sort.fz`, `enum_list_allocations.fz`, `enum_map_family.fz`, and `enum_reduce_suspend.fz` reflect fewer internal allocations/closures without hiding semantic suspend closures.

Strategy / DAG
1. Add the generalized runtime primitive and heap/list-cons mutation API.
2. Rename/generalize the shared IR physical capability model and delete old tail-only naming.
3. Make compiler2 native lowering birth and transport reusable-cons capabilities through hidden physical captures.
4. Make native codegen consume the generalized capability at all list-construction sites and remove the old tail-only codegen/runtime path.
5. Add telemetry and focused tests/fixture contracts that prove both semantics and allocation intent.
6. Update docs and remove obsolete fixture sidecars, old-world pins, names, and temporary affordances.

Non-goals
- Do not add a new semantic fact kind for physical reuse.
- Do not add shims/adapters bridging old `OwnedConsReuse` to a new model.
- Do not keep both tail-only and generalized runtime paths unless a live non-duplicative use exists and is justified in docs.
- Do not let sender-owned heap references cross receiver/process boundaries; process/message boundaries must block or copy, with runtime aliasing as the safety net.

comments.text:
Research / handoff anchors for this arc:

- FP2 paper: https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/research/wp-content/uploads/2023/05/fbip.pdf. Use it for the core principle: represent reuse/ownership credits explicitly and lower allocation into in-place update only when the dataflow proves the credit exists.
- Old-world inspiration, not implementation source of truth: src/ir_lower/cps.rs transports hidden owned-cons captures through continuations; src/ir_lower/cond.rs births captures from pattern/list-head extraction; src/ir_planner/return_context.rs and fn_types.rs show tuple-field return-demand specialization. Import the idea of physical-capability transport, not the planner/CPS sprawl or tail-only assumptions.
- Compiler2 seams to preserve: src/compiler2/identity.rs keeps ActivationKey semantic; src/fz_ir/mod.rs already erases physical_entry_params from semantic keys; src/compiler2/jobs/native.rs BackendStep::SplitList is capability birth; src/compiler2/native_codegen/prim.rs MakeList/DestListCons are capability consumption.
- Current observed failure mode: fz2 can run enum_sort semantically, but telemetry/allocation stats show internal over-allocation and heap-closure materialization. Use --log-telemetry with fixtures2/behavior/enum_sort.fz, enum_list_allocations.fz, enum_map_family.fz, and enum_reduce_suspend.fz while developing.
- Key design constraint: do not put reusable cons cells in semantic facts or ActivationKey. They are process-local physical capabilities. Runtime alias checks remain authoritative, so compiler facts are candidates, not uniqueness proofs.

title: Compiler2 physical reuse capabilities: reusable cons cells as ownership facts
